papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.abstract: We study empirical scaling laws for language model performance on the cross-entropy loss. The loss scales as a power-law with model size, dataset size, and the amount of compute used for training.

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms.

papers.title: SAM: Segment Anything Model
papers.abstract: We introduce the Segment Anything Model (SAM), a new task, model, and dataset for image segmentation. Using our model we achieve strong zero-shot transfer on a variety of segmentation tasks.

papers.title: DINO: DETR with Improved DeNoising
papers.abstract: We present DINO, a self-supervised method that combines the advantages of DINO and DETR. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art results on object detection and segmentation tasks.

papers.title: RLHF: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
papers.abstract: We use reinforcement learning from human feedback to train a simulator to mimic human labeling decisions, and then use this as a reward signal for fine-tuning language models.

papers.title: LLaMA: Open Foundation Models
papers.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets.

papers.title: GPT-4: Large Language Model for Science
papers.abstract: We present GPT-4, a large-scale, multimodal model which can accept image and text inputs and produce text outputs. GPT-4 exhibits human-level performance on various professional benchmarks.

papers.title: CLIP: Learning Transferable Visual Models
papers.abstract: We demonstrate that a simple pre-training task of predicting which image goes with which text is an efficient and scalable way to learn SOTA image representations from scratch.

papers.title: Diffusion Models: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive survey of diffusion models, covering the mathematical foundations, training methods, and applications in computer vision.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Object Detection: A Review
papers.abstract: Object detection is a fundamental computer vision task. This paper provides a comprehensive review of deep learning methods for object detection, from R-CNN to YOLO.

papers.title: Image Restoration with Deep Learning
papers.abstract: We propose a deep learning method for image restoration tasks including denoising, super-resolution, and deblurring. Our method achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks.

papers.title: Multimodal Learning: A Survey
papers.abstract: Multimodal learning is a promising approach to build models that can process and relate information from multiple modalities. This survey covers recent advances in multimodal learning.

papers.title: Visual Understanding with Deep Networks
papers.abstract: We study the mechanisms of visual cortex organization in mammals and show how these insights can guide the design of deep neural networks for visual understanding tasks.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning
papers.abstract: We explore how generating a chain of thought, a series of intermediate reasoning steps, significantly improves the ability of large language models to perform complex reasoning.

papers.title: Stable Diffusion: Text-to-Image Generation
papers.abstract: We present a latent text-to-image diffusion model that achieves competitive performance while significantly reducing computational requirements for training and inference.
